steps.description: Confirm /pay is the endpoint the claim targets and measure its p95
Decompose /pay span time and find which DB statements dominate
Isolate the orders.email lookup's share of /pay span time
Check what actually dominates /pay to size the alternative

dql: fetch spans, from: now() - 2h | filter request.is_root_span == true and service.name == "checkout" | summarize { reqs = count(), p95 = percentile(duration, 95) }, by: { endpoint.name } | sort p95 desc | limit 10
resultSummary: /pay is the slowest checkout endpoint at p95 4.2s

dql: fetch spans, from: now() - 2h | filter span.kind == "client" and isNotNull(db.system) and service.name == "checkout" | summarize { calls = count(), p95 = percentile(duration, 95) }, by: { db.statement } | sort p95 desc | limit 10
resultSummary: an orders(status, created_at) SELECT is 65% of /pay span time (~2.7s)

dql: fetch spans, from: now() - 2h | filter span.kind == "client" and isNotNull(db.system) and service.name == "checkout" and contains(db.statement, "orders.email") | summarize { calls = count(), total = sum(duration), p95 = percentile(duration, 95) }, by: { db.statement } | sort total desc
resultSummary: the orders.email lookup is only ~4% of /pay span time (~0.17s)

dql: fetch spans, from: now() - 2h | filter span.kind == "client" and isNotNull(db.system) and service.name == "checkout" and contains(db.statement, "orders") and contains(db.statement, "status") | summarize { calls = count(), p95 = percentile(duration, 95) }, by: { db.statement } | sort p95 desc | limit 5
resultSummary: the dominant cost is an unindexed sequential scan on orders(status, created_at), not email

verdict.whySmall: the orders.email lookup is only ~4% of /pay span time
verdict.confidenceReason: the email lookup's share of /pay span time was measured directly from spans over the last 2h
verdict.dissent: under a future email-heavy lookup pattern (e.g. a new account-merge flow) the index could matter more
verdict.recommendedAction: do not add the orders.email index now — it returns ~0.1s; instead index orders(status, created_at)

evidence.label: orders.email share of /pay
evidence.dql: fetch spans, from: now() - 2h | filter span.kind == "client" and isNotNull(db.system) and service.name == "checkout" and contains(db.statement, "orders.email") | summarize { total = sum(duration), p95 = percentile(duration, 95) }, by: { db.statement }

evidence.label: true /pay hotspot
evidence.resultSummary: orders(status, created_at) sequential scan = 65% of /pay p95 (~2.7s)
evidence.dql: fetch spans, from: now() - 2h | filter span.kind == "client" and isNotNull(db.system) and service.name == "checkout" | summarize { p95 = percentile(duration, 95) }, by: { db.statement } | sort p95 desc | limit 5

redirect.finding: 65% of /pay p95 is an unindexed sequential scan on orders(status, created_at).
